DECLARATION OF JOHN C. WON IN SUPPORT O FDEFT'S RESPONSE TO PLTF'S OPPOS TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICAITON FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT SHAPIRO, ALEX SHAPIRO, MARGARITA SHAPIRO, LEV

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALEX SHAPIRO, MARGARITA SHAPIRO, AND, LEV SHAPIRO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY AJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEFENDANTS DID NOT OWE PLAINTIFF A DUTY AS A MATTER OF LAW. NO SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP EXISTED BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS. THE HARM TO PLAINTIFF WAS NOT REASONABLY FORESEEABLE. YAGIAN WAS NOT INVITED TO THE PARTY AND NO COPY OF MY SPACE PAGE PROVIDED. (SEE MARGARET W. VS. KELLY R. (2006) 139 CAL.APP. 4TH 141.) THE PARTY DID NOT CONSTITUTE A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PROPERTY AND IT WAS NOT FORESEEABLE THAT YAGIAN WOULD APPEAR AT THE PARTY, OR THAT HE WOULD HAVE AN INTENTION TO ASSAULT. (SEE ERIC J. VS. BETTY M. (1999) 76 CAL.APP. 4TH 715.) NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANTS EVER COMMUNICATED TO PLAINTIFF TO SUPPORT THE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION. (DEFTS. OBJECT. TO EVID. FILED AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT HEARING.)ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF KAZARYAN, KEVIN TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT SHAPIRO, ALEX SHAPIRO, MARGARITA SHAPIRO, LEV SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ALEX SHAPIRO, MARGARITA SHAPIRO, and, LEV SHAPIRO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY AJUDICATION. Appearances: Burke Hansen, Law Offices of Burke Hansen, (415) 771-6174, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; John C. Wong, of Haapala, Thompson & Abern LLP (510) 763-2324, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendants shift the burden of proof and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Defendants did not owe plaintiff a duty as a matter of law. No special relationship existed between plaintiff and defendants. The harm to plaintiff was not reasonably foreseeable. Yagian was not invited to the party and no copy of My Space Page provided. (See Margaret W. vs. Kelly R. (2006) 139 Cal.App. 4th 141.) The party did not constitute a dangerous condition of property and it was not foreseeable that Yagian would appear at the party, or that he would have an intention to assault. (See Eric J. vs. Betty M. (1999) 76 Cal.App. 4th 715.) No evidence that defendants ever communicated to plaintiff to support the negligent misrepresentation cause of action. (Defts. Object. to Evid. filed and copies given to counsel at hearing.)Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
